text: To go pear-shaped
A hiccup
To keep your cool
A curveball
To think on your feet
A contingency plan
A backup plan
To fall through
To make do
To roll with the punches
To take it in your stride
To weather the storm
To be caught off guard
A worst-case scenario
We will just roll with the punches.
She took the delay in her stride.
The bad news caught me off guard.
If I lost my passport, I would go to the embassy.
If the flight were cancelled, I would find another route.
What would you do if you missed your connection?
If I were you, I would stay calm.
I cannot believe it. The flight is cancelled, and the hotel has no record of my booking. Everything has gone pear-shaped at once.
Take a breath. It is just a hiccup. If I were you, I would keep my cool and deal with it one piece at a time.
You are right. But what would you do if you had no room for the night?
I would make do with the airport tonight and build a contingency plan. If the cheaper hotel fell through too, I would have a backup plan ready.
That actually helps. I was caught off guard, but if I thought of it as a puzzle, I think I could roll with the punches.
Exactly. Take it in your stride. By morning we will have weathered the storm, and this will be the best story of your trip.

I want to tell you about the night a perfect trip completely fell through. I was changing planes in a city I had never been to, and within an hour, everything went pear-shaped. My connection was cancelled, the airline had lost my onward booking, and my hotel had given my room to someone else. For a few minutes I just froze; I honestly could not think on my feet at all. But then I made a choice. I decided to keep my cool and treat it as one big puzzle, not one big disaster. I made do with a quiet corner and a phone charger, and I built a quick contingency plan: a new route home, a cheaper hotel nearby, and a backup plan in case that one fell through too. By sunrise I had weathered the storm. And here is the strange part: that curveball became the best story of the whole trip. If it happened again, I honestly would not panic. I would take it in my stride, because now I know I can roll with the punches.

Hi Pricila, it's me, leaving you a quick voice note before your big trip next month. I just want to give you one piece of advice, traveller to traveller. Something will probably go wrong at some point. A flight gets delayed, a booking disappears, the weather turns. It always does, and that's fine. If I were you, I wouldn't see it as a disaster. I would treat every hiccup as part of the adventure. The travellers who enjoy themselves the most are not the ones with perfect plans; they're the ones who keep their cool and roll with the punches. So before you leave, think about your worst-case scenario for a minute, make a simple backup plan, and then forget about it. If something falls through while you're away, you'll think on your feet and handle it. And if you ever feel caught off guard, just breathe, take it in your stride, and remember: the best stories almost always come from the moments that did not go as planned. Have an amazing trip.
